publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 30, 2024 at 02:00

did.unittitle: Publications on fluoridation

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: Fluoridation of public water supplies to promote dental health in the United States began in 1945 and was endorsed by the American Dental Association in 1950. The collection includes pamphlets, reprints, reports, and correspondence evaluating and promoting fluoridation of water created from 1947-1962. Many of the materials in the collection were produced by the ADA.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Collection was created by Medical Historical Library staff. Provenance of items in the collection is unknown.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The American Dental Association (ADA) was formed in 1859 in Niagara Falls, New York to promote good dental health. The headquarters of the organization are located in Chicago, Illinois. The ADA lobbies for policies related to dental care, provides accreditation for dental care professionals, and tests and endorses dental products.

Fluoridation of public water supplies to promote dental health in the United States began in 1945. The ADA endorsed the fluoridation of water in 1950, after which they began producing printed materials supporting the practice.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Publications on fluoridation. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fluoridation of public water supplies to promote dental health in the United States began in 1945 and was endorsed by the American Dental Association in 1950. The collection includes pamphlets, reprints, reports, and correspondence evaluating and promoting fluoridation of water created from 1947-1962.
Series I. includes materials produced by the ADA and other organizations between 1951-1953. Materials in this series were organized by library staff at an unknown time.

Series II. includes materials produced by the ADA in 1953 or later and materials produced by other organizations between 1947-1962. Materials in this series include items organized by the library in 1953 and additional items removed from undescribed public health collections in 2024.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: American Dental Association 1953 information packet
American Dental Association, Why your dentist recommends fluoridation . Chicago, Illinois, 1952.
American Dental Association, "Excerpts of letters from authorities in areas where fluorides occur naturally or are being added to the public water supply". 1953.
American Dental Association, Fluoridation facts: answers to criticism of fluoridation . Chicago, Illinois, 1953.
American Dental Association, Fluoridation in the prevention of dental caries , second edition. Chicago, Illinois, 1953.
American Dental Association, "Resolution regarding fluoridation of community water supplies adopted by the House of Delegates of the American Dental Association". 1953.
American Dental Association, "Resources for information and visual materials on fluoridation". 1953.
American Dental Association, Council on Dental Health, "Status of fluoridation in the United States, its territories and possessions". 1953.
American Medical Association, Fluoridation of drinking water harmless: statement by the American Medical Association , reprinted from The Journal of the American Dental Association , vol. 43. 1951.
American Medical Association, "Position of the American Medical Association on the fluoridation of public water supplies". 1953.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attached to letter from Dr. George F. Lull to Dr. Harold Hillenbrand.

did.unittitle: American Water Works Association, British fluoridation mission report , Willing Water , no. 23, August 1953. New York City, New York, 1953.

Arnold, Francis A., H. Trendley Dean, John W. Knutson, Effect of fluoridated public water supplies on water supplies on dental caries prevalence , Public Health Reports , reprint no. 3219, vol. 68, no. 2. U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, 1953.

Ast, David B., Helen C. Chase, The Newburgh-Kingston caries fluorine study iv. dental findings after six years of water fluoridation , reprinted from Oral Surgery, Oral Medicine, and Oral Pathology , vol. 6, no. 1. 1953.

Cox, Gerald J., Harold C. Hodge, The toxicity of fluorides in relation to their use in dentistry , reprinted from The Journal of the American Dental Association , vol. 40. American Dental Association, 1950.
Doty, J. Roy, W. Philip Phair, An analysis of the Delaney Committee report on the fluoridation of drinking water , reprinted from The Journal of the American Dental Association , vol. 45. 1952.

Doty, J. Roy, The irresponsible opposition to fluoridation , The
Journal of the American Dental Association , reprinted from vol. 47. 1953.
Furnas, J.C., The magic of fluorine: the new way to reduce tooth decay . New York City, New York: American Dental Association, circa 1953.

Heyroth, Francis F., Toxicological evidence for the safety of the fluoridation of public water supplies , reprinted from American Journal of Public Health , vol. 42, no. 12. New York City, New York: American Public Health Association, Inc., 1952.
Inter-Association Committee on Health. Inter-Association Committee on Health statement on fluoridation , reprinted from The Journal of the American Dental Associati on, vol. 44. 1952.
Johnson, Dorothy M., Can we give our children better teeth? . American Dental Association, 1953.
Lee, Henry, A sure way to reduce tooth decay , Pageant , May 1953. Hillman Periodicals, Inc., 1953.
National Academy of Sciences, National Research Council, Division of Medical Sciences, Report of the ad hoc committee on fluoridation of water supplies . Washington, D.C., 1952.

Phair, W. Philip, Marian F. Driscoll, The status of fluoridation programs in the United States, its territories and possessions , reprinted from The Journal of the American Dental Association , vol. 45. 1952.
Other materials
American Dental Association, Bureau of Public Information, "Comments on the opponents of fluoridation". Chicago, Illinois, 1956.
Commission on Chronic Illness, Fluoridation of water supplies endorsed by commission at annual meeting , Chronic Illness News Letter , vol. 5, no. 4. Baltimore, Maryland, 1954.
Connecticut State Department of Health, Fluorine and its relationship to dental health (abstracts) . Hartford, Connecticut, 1947.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes insert about fluorine mouth wash.

did.unittitle: Connecticut State Department of Health, Drink away tomorrow's tooth decay . Connecticut, n.d.

Cox, Gerald J., Harold C. Hodge, The toxicity of fluorides in relation to their use in dentistry , reprinted from the Journal of the American Dental Association , vol. 40. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service, 1954.
Federal Security Agency, Public Health Service, New discovery curbs tooth decay . Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, n.d.
Galagan, Donald J., Climate and controlled fluoridation , reprinted from the Journal of the American Dental Association, vol. 47, August 1953. 1953.
"Fluoridation..........the official position of national organizations." 1956.

Hagan, Thomas L., Morton Pasternack, Grace C. Scholz, Waterbourne fluorides and mortality , Public Health Reports, reprint no. 3232, vol. 69, no. 5, May 1954. Washington, D.C.: U.S Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service, 1954.

Hilleboe, Herman E., Edward R. Schlesinger, Helen C. Chase, Katherine T. Cantwell, David B. Ast, David J. Smith, Barnet Wachs, David E. Overton, Harold C. Hodge, Newburgh-Kingston caries-fluorine study: final report , reprinted from the Journal of the American Dental Association, vol. 52, March 1956. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service, 1956.

Law, Frank E., Water fluoridation---a positive health protection , reprinted from California's Health, September 15, 1956. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service, 1956.

Leone, Nicholas C., Michael B. Shimkin, Francis A. Arnold, Jr., Clyde A. Stevenson, Eugene R. Zimmermann, Patricia B. Geiser, Jacob E. Lieberman, Medical aspects of excessive fluoride in a water supply , Public Health Reports reprint no. 3242, vol. 69, no. 10. Washington, D.C., 1954.

Leone, Nicholas C., Erving F. Geever, Neil C. Moran, Acute and subacute toxicity studies of sodium fluoride in animals , from Public Health Reports , vol. 71, no.5. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service. Washington, D.C., 1956.

McClure, F.J., Nondental physiological effects of trace quantities of fluorine , reprinted from Dental caries and fluorine . Washington, D.C., U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service. Washington, D.C., circa 1958.
National Academy of Sciences, National Research Council, Division of Medical Sciences, Report of the ad hoc committee on fluoridation of water supplies , publication 214. Washington, D.C., 1952.

National Academy of Sciences, National Research Council, Division of Biology and Agriculture, Food and Nutrition Board, The problem of providing optimum fluoride intake for prevention of dental caries , publication 294. Washington, D.C., 1953.
National Security Agency, Food and Drug Administration, Fluorides in foods . Washington, D.C., 1952.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes data prepared by the National Canners Association.

did.unittitle: New Jersey Public Health Association, Notes on fluoridation . Trenton, New Jersey, 1962.
Board of Health of the City of New York, Report to the mayor on fluoridation for New York City . New York City, New York, 1955.

Pelton, Walter J., Elliott H. Pennell, Anton Druzina, Tooth morbidity experience of adults , reprinted from the Journal of the American Dental Association , vol., 49, October 1954. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service, 1954.

Russell, A.L., Elias Elvove, Domestic water and dental caries: vii. a study of the fluoride-dental caries relationship in an adult population , Public Health Reports reprint no. 3120. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service, 1954.
Steinberg, Charles Leroy, Dwight E. Gardner, Frank A. Smith, Harold C. Hodge, Fluoridation of public water supplies and its relationship to muscularskeletal diseases . Bethesda, Maryland, 1956.
Bureau of Municipal Research Syracuse, New York, American communities reported adopting, then rescinding fluoridation (including two which have reinstated fluoridation) . Syracuse, New York, 1956.

Zipkin, I., R.C. Likins, F.J. McClure, A.C. Steere, Urinary fluoride levels associated with use of fluoridated waters , Public Health Reports , vol. 71, no. 8, August 1956. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Public Health Service, 1956.
